text: One of the most frequent challenges during redo procedures is the presence of adhesions.

Thanks to the many technological advantages offered by the robotic system, such as the ten-time MADEC of eye vision, free-D vision, tremor filtration and seven degrees of freedom movement of the instruments, it is possible to achieve a well-defined and safe adhesiolysis, even in narrow spaces or generally difficult settings such as the chest cavity.
The surgeon carefully separates the parenchyma from the parietal pleura using bipolar forceps and a monopolar hook, demonstrating precise tissue manipulation.
The robotic system's technological advantages assist in the adhesiolysis between the lung parenchyma and major vessels, including the aorta, as visible in this segment of the video.
